insulin_sufficiency.summary: Overall insulin delivery shows opportunity for improvement (TIR 58%, TBR<70 2.0%, TAR>180 40%). 2 risk(s) identified; 1 parameter change(s) proposed this cycle.

basal.evidence: Deconfounded basal block audit (EXP-3447): morning 6:00-12:00 has 4082 clean samples after inferred-meal exclusion, median glucose 152 mg/dL, TBR 1.7%, and Loop actual/scheduled basal median 1.24 with additions >115% in 55% of samples. [Controller: Loop]
basal.justification: Basal increase: implement the practical step 1.7 -> 1.87 (+10%) now. Theoretical optimum is 1.87 (+10%); the practical step honors a safe titration cap of 20% per cycle. This derives from a validated deconfounded estimate (the controller-masking confound is already removed), so it is not down-weighted for controller masking. Increase basal by 10% during morning (6:00-12:00). This is a narrow deconfounded step: inferred-meal windows are excluded, Loop is consistently adding basal, and TBR is below 4%. Use the 2-week TBR guardrail before considering another step.

success: TIR improves by at least +0.6 pp toward the projected 59% within 2 weeks.
TBR<70 does not worsen by more than 1 pp.
stop_escalate: TBR<70 increases by more than 1 pp after the Basal change -> revert and escalate.
No measurable TIR improvement at 2 weeks -> reassess direction.

isf.evidence: No actionable ISF signal in the analysis window; current setting is consistent with observed glucose behavior.
isf.justification: ISF reviewed: the data do not support a change this cycle. Maintaining the current setting and re-evaluating at the next review.

success: ISF held: glycemic metrics remain within tolerance of baseline over the 2-week window.
No new hypoglycemia signal (TBR<70 stays below 4%).
stop_escalate: TBR<70 rises by more than 1 pp -> escalate review.
TIR declines materially -> re-open ISF for change.

cr.evidence: No actionable Carb ratio signal in the analysis window; current setting is consistent with observed glucose behavior.
cr.justification: Carb ratio reviewed: the data do not support a change this cycle. Maintaining the current setting and re-evaluating at the next review.

success: Carb ratio held: glycemic metrics remain within tolerance of baseline over the 2-week window.
No new hypoglycemia signal (TBR<70 stays below 4%).
stop_escalate: TBR<70 rises by more than 1 pp -> escalate review.
TIR declines materially -> re-open Carb ratio for change.

overall_justification: Practical changes this cycle: Basal 1.7->1.87. Each practical step is the safe-titration projection of a larger theoretical optimum (documented in the addenda). Held/deferred: ISF (no_meaningful_deviation), Carb ratio (no_meaningful_deviation).
addenda: Factors considered: time-in-range distribution, hypo/hyper burden, glycemic variability, per-parameter advisory evidence, and cross-parameter sequencing.
Basal theoretical optimum: 1.87 (+10% vs current 1.7); practical step capped at 20%/cycle -> 1.87.
Risks reviewed and mitigated: Hyperglycemia burden elevated (TAR>180 40.1% vs target 25%). Glycemic variability high (CV 37% vs target 36%).

Deconfounding applied: Basal recommendation(s) derive from validated deconfounded estimates (e.g. demand-phase ISF EXP-2651, correction-denominator/bilateral EXP-2741, deconfounded basal EXP-3447). The controller-masking confidence penalty does not apply to these, improving accuracy without removing the controller's residual safety margin (EXP-2738).

Mitigations: changes are bounded by a per-cycle titration cap; carb ratio is sequenced after basal/ISF to avoid confounded adjustment; explicit stop/escalate criteria accompany every recommendation for the 2-week feedback loop.

Dose-shaping guidance (ISF, NOT a baseline schedule change): Correction doses above 1.5U show diminishing returns. At 2.8U, each unit achieves only 16 mg/dL drop vs 40 mg/dL at 1U. Consider: (1) splitting large corrections into smaller doses spaced 30+ min apart, (2) using ISF=16 for doses ≥3U. This is a pharmacokinetic property (β=0.9), not circadian. This describes a dose-conditional effective value for large corrections; prefer splitting large corrections over altering the baseline ISF schedule.

reboot.rationale: Settings reboot not indicated: the severe-mismatch composite (burden + large mismatch + low consistency) is not met. Incremental titration is appropriate.

reimbursement.data_sufficiency: Analysis based on 90 days of CGM data (23,948 readings). Sufficient for time-in-range and titration assessment.
mitigations: Recommendations bounded by a safe per-cycle titration cap.
Carb ratio sequenced after basal/ISF to prevent confounded change.
Explicit stop/escalate criteria defined for each recommendation.
alternatives_discussed: Considered no-change vs incremental titration vs settings reboot.
Theoretical optima documented but deferred in favor of safe steps.
patient_barriers: No patient-reported adherence, supply, or prescription barriers noted at this review.
reimbursement.agreed_plan: Agreed plan: Basal -> 1.87. Re-evaluate in 2 weeks.
reimbursement.expected_trajectory: Projected time-in-range at next review: ~59% (baseline 58%). Outcome will be scored against the per-recommendation success and stop/escalate criteria.

figures.title: Time-in-range distribution
figures.caption: Share of time in each glycemic band over 23,948 readings. Green is the 70-180 mg/dL target; reds are lows, ambers are highs.
figures.alt: Stacked bar of time spent in each glucose range.

figures.title: Ambulatory glucose profile (AGP)
figures.caption: Median glucose by time of day with interquartile (25–75%) and 10–90% bands. The green zone is the 70–180 mg/dL target; a flat median inside it is the goal.
figures.alt: Ambulatory glucose profile percentile bands by hour of day.

figures.title: Overnight glucose profile (00:00–06:00)
figures.caption: Median overnight glucose with IQR. A rising trend suggests basal is too low; a falling trend suggests it is too high. Used to contextualize the basal recommendation.
figures.alt: Overnight median glucose by hour with interquartile band.

figures.title: Post-meal glucose excursion
figures.caption: Median glucose rise after 5 carb-counted, bolused meals (peak +45 mg/dL at 45 min; -108 mg/dL vs baseline at 4 h). Carb ratio held this cycle; this profile is the baseline to compare against at the next review.
figures.alt: Mean post-meal glucose excursion curve with interquartile band.

figures.title: Scheduled vs actual basal
figures.caption: Median programmed basal vs what the loop actually delivered by hour. Persistent loop deviation indicates the scheduled rate is mismatched in that direction.

figures.title: ISF reconciliation (profile vs observed)
figures.caption: Profile ISF vs the correction-derived (observed/apparent) ISF. The observed value is amplified by AID compensation (basal suspension during corrections), so it is NOT a direct ISF target: the recommendation deliberately preserves the controller's residual safety margin (EXP-2738) rather than chasing the apparent value. Separately, a lower effective ISF for large single corrections is dose-shaping guidance (split the dose), not a baseline schedule change.
